perturbation.original_text: 6. Breaches 6.1 A breach of this Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest Agreement may result in action being taken against the member (up to and including removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee). 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Breaches 6.1 A breach of this Confidentiality and Conflict of Interest Agreement may result in action being taken against the member (up to and including a warning). 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the potential consequences of a breach to only 'a warning' in section 6.1, we create an In-Text contradiction with section 6.2, which contemplates 'removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee', contradicting 6.1 where it sets warning as the limited punishment. Now the punishment is ambiguos.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 In the case of a breach by a Board or Committee Member, the Board will determine what steps should be taken under the circumstances (provided that consid eration of the removal of a Board member shall be on thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember. After thirty (30) days notice to the Board M ember, the Board, may remove the person from the Board through the applicable provisions and procedures created by the SAMED Constitution.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that a breach could result in 'action being taken against the member (up to and including removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee)'. The changed text limits the potential consequences of a breach to only 'a warning'. This directly contradicts section 6.2 (which is also the contradicted text), which contemplates 'removal from the Board and/or SAMED Committee'. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction as it introduces ambiguity about the possible punishments for a breach, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. This adheres to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Terms 7.1 This agreement shall commence upon the date of signature by both parties (“the effective date") to this agreement and shall continue to bind the parties for the duration of one year . A new agreement must be signed should a person be re -elected or re - appointed as a Board - and/or Committee member.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Terms 7.1 This agreement shall commence upon the date of signature by both parties (“the effective date") to this agreement and shall continue to bind the parties until terminated by either party with 30 days written notice. A new agreement must be signed should a person be re -elected or re - appointed as a Board - and/or Committee member.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'until terminated by either party with 30 days written notice' introduces an In-Text contradiction. It now conflicts with the initial term of 'one year', because the agreement can be breached at anytime, and is not only limited to one year. The clause creates an uncertainty on the term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Terms 7.1 This agreement shall commence upon the date of signature by both parties (“the effective date") to this agreement and shall continue to bind the parties until terminated by either party with 30 days written notice. A new agreement must be signed should a person be re -elected or re - appointed as a Board - and/or Committee member.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the term of the agreement. Originally, it was for 'one year'. The changed text allows for termination by either party with 30 days' notice, which contradicts the fixed 'one year' term. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case. Thus, there is a direct in-text contradiction because the contract now specifies both a fixed term and a termination clause that allows for ending the agreement before the fixed term expires.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Additional action 8.1 Each party to this agreement shall execute and deliver such other documents and do such other acts and things as may be necessary or desirable to give effect to the terms and provisions of this agreement. This may include the return and/or distraction of documents, information, files, emails and the like that came to be in his/her possession during his/her tenure as a Board - and/or Committee member, upon resignation or removal from such a position.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Additional action 8.1 Each party to this agreement may, at their sole discretion, execute and deliver such other documents and do such other acts and things as may be necessary or desirable to give effect to the terms and provisions of this agreement. This may include the return and/or distraction of documents, information, files, emails and the like that came to be in his/her possession during his/her tenure as a Board - and/or Committee member, upon resignation or removal from such a position.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall execute' to 'may, at their sole discretion, execute', we create an In-Text contradiction. The original text mandates additional actions to fulfill the agreement. The modified version makes these actions optional, making it uncertain if either party actually needs to adhere to these 'additional actions'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Additional action 8.1 Each party to this agreement may, at their sole discretion, execute and deliver such other documents and do such other acts and things as may be necessary or desirable to give effect to the terms and provisions of this agreement. This may include the return and/or distraction of documents, information, files, emails and the like that came to be in his/her possession during his/her tenure as a Board - and/or Committee member, upon resignation or removal from such a position.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Each party to this agreement shall execute' which creates a mandatory obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'Each party to this agreement may, at their sole discretion, execute,' which makes the execution of documents optional. This directly contradicts the implication of the original 'shall' statement, creating uncertainty and a strong contradiction as to whether the parties are actually required to perform the actions outlined in that section. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because it makes enforcement difficult by introducing ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Undertaking Respecting Confidentiality and Con flicts of Interest Whereas , and based on the above rules, from time to time, arising out of the relationship between the undersigned and the Board and/or any Committee of SAMED, the undersigned shall come into possession or have knowledge of certain matters, information and things which are confidential, i.e. “confidential information and s/he may be in situations of conflict as outlined in the aforementioned provisions, therefore the person declares as follows: I have read the above undertakings and certify that I fully understand the nature and effect of these undertakings, and acknowledge receipt of a copy thereof. I further agree to be bound by the rules of confidentiality and conflict of interest as set out above. I also understand the need for these requirements and I believe I can meet the requirements as set out above. Signed at on For Board / Committee Member: Full names and surname of Signature For SAMED: Full names and surname Signature
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Undertaking Respecting Confidentiality and Con flicts of Interest Whereas , and based on the above rules, from time to time, arising out of the relationship between the undersigned and the Board and/or any Committee of SAMED, the undersigned shall come into possession or have knowledge of certain matters, information and things which are confidential, i.e. “confidential information and s/he may be in situations of conflict as outlined in the aforementioned provisions, therefore the person declares as follows: I have read the above undertakings and certify that I fully understand the nature and effect of these undertakings, and acknowledge receipt of a copy thereof. I further agree to be bound by the rules of confidentiality and conflict of interest as set out above, but with the understanding that SAMED's best interests will always be prioritized. I also understand the need for these requirements and I believe I can meet the requirements as set out above. Signed at on For Board / Committee Member: Full names and surname of Signature For SAMED: Full names and surname Signature
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'but with the understanding that SAMED's best interests will always be prioritized' to the final undertaking creates an In-Text contradiction. This addition allows for prioritizing SAMED's interests, which could conflict with the individual's obligations under confidentiality and conflict of interest rules, leading to uncertainty in enforcement. If an indivual believes they can follow conflict of interest, rules, but will prioritize SAMED's intrest, then what is the point of following rules.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. Undertaking Respecting Confidentiality and Con flicts of Interest Whereas , and based on the above rules, from time to time, arising out of the relationship between the undersigned and the Board and/or any Committee of SAMED, the undersigned shall come into possession or have knowledge of certain matters, information and things which are confidential, i.e. “confidential information and s/he may be in situations of conflict as outlined in the aforementioned provisions, therefore the person declares as follows: I have read the above undertakings and certify that I fully understand the nature and effect of these undertakings, and acknowledge receipt of a copy thereof. I further agree to be bound by the rules of confidentiality and conflict of interest as set out above, but with the understanding that SAMED's best interests will always be prioritized. I also understand the need for these requirements and I believe I can meet the requirements as set out above. Signed at on For Board / Committee Member: Full names and surname of Signature For SAMED: Full names and surname Signature
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction within Section 13. While the original text emphasizes adherence to confidentiality and conflict of interest rules, the changed text adds 'but with the understanding that SAMED's best interests will always be prioritized.' This addition creates a direct conflict because prioritizing SAMED's interests could lead to breaches of confidentiality or violations of conflict of interest rules, which the individual initially agreed to uphold. This creates an In-Text Contradiction as per the Perturbation Explanation, and falls under the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
